items.headline: City launching amnesty program for unlicensed businesses
items.summary: Cupertino is rolling out a business license amnesty program targeting shopping centers, allowing businesses to get licensed without penalties for past violations. This could bring previously under-the-radar retailers into compliance while helping the city collect taxes and fees.

items.headline: New economic incentives for retail and small startups
items.summary: The city is introducing incentive programs to attract and support retail stores and startup businesses in Cupertino. This signals an effort to diversify the local economy beyond tech and revitalize commercial areas.

items.headline: Mixed-use housing project approved at 490 East Middlefield Road
items.summary: The City Council approved a development agreement for a mixed-use residential development at 490 East Middlefield Road on the second reading, moving the project toward construction. This represents new housing supply in Mountain View.

items.headline: Mountain View applies for state housing incentive funding
items.summary: The city submitted an application for Round 4 of California's Prohousing Incentive Program, which provides grants to cities that remove barriers to housing development. If awarded, the funding could help accelerate local housing projects.

items.headline: City seeking public input on $HUD housing funds for next year
items.summary: Santa Clara is accepting public comment on how to spend federal HUD grant money for housing programs in fiscal year 2026/27. This is your chance to weigh in on housing priorities before the city finalizes its plan.

items.headline: City holding safe parking community meeting
items.summary: Sunnyvale is hosting a community meeting on Monday, March 23 at 5:30 p.m. to discuss safe parking options, likely related to vehicle dwelling or homeless services. This is a chance for residents to learn about or weigh in on the city's approach to a visible issue in the South Bay.
